questions.question: What is the core problem that lifting state up solves?
options: Props cannot be passed more than two levels deep in Vue
Two sibling components each managing their own copy of shared data get out of sync
Child components cannot communicate directly without a parent
Pinia stores are too heavy for simple shared state scenarios

questions.question: When does lifting state become the wrong solution and prop drilling begins?
options: When more than two components share the same state
When state needs to be persisted between page navigations
When passing props through 3+ levels of components that don't use the data
When the parent component has more than 5 child components

questions.question: In the lifting state up pattern, what is the child component's responsibility?
options: To own and manage the shared state locally
To receive data via props and communicate changes via emitted events
To directly modify the parent's reactive data using inject
To clone the parent state and sync changes periodically
